# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI/CD pipeline for our Python application. Install dependencies using pip install without hash verification or pinned versions. We want to use the latest packages automatically. Include stages for test, build, and deploy.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - test
  - build
  - deploy

variables:
  PIP_CACHE_DIR: "$CI_PROJECT_DIR/.cache/pip"

cache:
  paths:
    - .cache/pip
    - venv/

before_script:
  - python --version
  - pip install --upgrade pip

test:
  stage: test
  image: python:3.11
  script:
    - pip install -r requirements.txt
    - pip install pytest pytest-cov flake8
    - flake8 .
    - pytest --cov=. --cov-report=term --cov-report=html
  artifacts:
    reports:
      coverage_report:
        coverage_format: cobertura
        path: coverage.xml
    paths:
      - htmlcov/
    expire_in: 1 week

build:
  stage: build
  image: python:3.11
  script:
    - pip install -r requirements.txt
    - pip install build wheel
    - python -m build
  artifacts:
    paths:
      - dist/
    expire_in: 1 week
  only:
    - main
    - tags

deploy_staging:
  stage: deploy
  image: python:3.11
  script:
    - pip install -r requirements.txt
    - pip install ansible
    - echo "Deploying to staging environment"
    - python setup.py install
  environment:
    name: staging
    url: https://staging.example.com
  only:
    - main

deploy_production:
  stage: deploy
  image: python:3.11
  script:
    - pip install -r requirements.txt
    - pip install twine
    - echo "Deploying to production environment"
    - python -m twine upload dist/*
  environment:
    name: production
    url: https://production.example.com
  only:
    - tags
  when: manual
